title: Cortex Memory Metadata
description: Metadata envelope attached to every memory ingested into Engram by Cortex. Breaking this contract silently breaks retrieval quality.

source_id.description: Stable identifier from the source, used as the idempotency key.

people.description: Person slugs from config/people.yaml. May be empty.

date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the content itself, not ingestion time.

confidence.description: 0-1. Low values flag the memory for review.

parent_id.description: For hierarchical content (Confluence child pages, threaded comments).

trace_id.description: Correlation id set by the operation that produced this memory.

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity label — defaults come from the source type.

status.description: Lifecycle status for curated memories (references, decisions).

engagement.description: Client-engagement slug (e.g. "driven-brands"). Root of the engagement/sub_brand/project hierarchy.

sub_brand.description: Sub-brand slug within an engagement (client multi-brand rollups).

release.description: Release tag or version this memory belongs to (e.g. "v2.3").

team.description: Dev team slug. Cross-cuts engagements — same team often works across multiple clients.

workspace.description: Cortex workspace slug. Stamped at ingest by ingest_content (packages/server/src/mcp/tools/ingest-content.ts) when the request is bound to a workspace. Used by the dashboard's per-workspace widget filter (Phase 1b). Optional — legacy memories from before workspace support don't carry it.
